own_review_points: Calendar-based TIME/TIMESTAMP path: should the calendar pass through as-is rather than be reconstructed? (human ref)
Calendar path may need an explicit 'Z'/offset to force offset-based timestamp format when jdbcTimeZone is null (human ref)
TIME handling: timezone is meaningless for a time-of-day; simplified Instant.EPOCH-based construction preferred (human ref)
appendLiteral backslash-escaping correctness for Spanner (comment justifies it; not a clear defect)
TIME rendered as TIMESTAMP with offset - absolute-point-in-time semantics worth documenting
currentTime() delegates to currentTimestamp() - returns full timestamp not time-of-day
notes: Every one of v8's six points is hedged with 'please confirm', 'worth noting', 'worth documenting', or 'no issue, just noting' - none asserts an actual defect, and several explicitly conclude the code is correct. This is a diff-restating review with no real findings. It also missed all of the maintainer's substantive feedback on the Calendar-based literal path (pass-through, forcing the offset/'Z', and the simplified time construction). Net clearly negative.
